bridge_issues.issue: Calls invoke() from @tauri-apps/api/core directly without an isTauri() guard. In web mode the real Tauri invoke will be invoked, which will throw because there is no Tauri backend. Mitigation: file is never imported by production code (only by env-bridge-polyfill.ts which itself has no production callers), so this is currently latent but would activate if either file is wired up for web mode.

File carries a TODO comment marking it as a dead module (no production imports). It duplicates types from rust-ipc.ts (AgentSession, ToolInfo). Should be deleted once tauri-agent.test.ts tests are migrated to use-rust-agent.ts.

findings.issue: Tauri invoke() called without isTauri() guard. In web mode this will throw. Currently safe because the file has no production callers, but represents a latent web-mode regression if wired up.
Dead module kept only for unit tests (tauri-agent.test.ts). Duplicates RustSession/RustToolInfo types from rust-ipc.ts. Candidate for deletion once tests are migrated to use-rust-agent.ts.

notes.debug_logs: Zero debug logs matching console.warn.*THINKING-DEBUG, console.warn.*DEBUG, or console.log.*DEBUG found in src/.
notes.broken_imports: Zero imports from 'tauri-agent' found in production code. Zero React imports (useState, useEffect, from 'react') found anywhere in src/.
notes.isTauri_branching: rust-bridge.ts uses isTauri() consistently: Tauri path calls tauriInvoke<T>, web path falls through to apiInvoke<T>. All other service files (plugins-fs.ts, git-extension.ts, file-browser.ts, db-init.ts, etc.) correctly import isTauri from @tauri-apps/api/core and guard Tauri-only code paths.
notes.rust_bridge: rust-bridge.ts is clean. invokeCommand() is the single dispatch function with correct Tauri/web branching. rustBackend, rustAgent (legacy alias), rustMemory, rustPermissions, rustValidation, rustGit, rustBrowser, rustTools, rustCompute, rustReflection, rustPty, rustOAuth, rustSystem, rustPlugins, rustExtensions all funnel through invokeCommand(). rustAgent is a legacy export that duplicates a subset of rustBackend — callers (useAgent.ts) already use the rustAgent alias cleanly.
notes.store_health: session store (src/stores/session/): all signals initialized in createRoot(), busySessionIds safely guards window.addEventListener with typeof window check, localStorage reads guarded with typeof localStorage check. settings store (src/stores/settings/): signals initialized in createRoot(), all exports are valid re-exports with no missing symbols.
notes.env_bridge_polyfill: env-bridge-polyfill.ts has zero production callers in src/ — confirmed by grep. The latent isTauri-missing-guard issue in env-bridge.ts cannot be triggered by current code paths.
